response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep, senior-level C++ expertise as its core competency, yet his C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor's thesis project. His career trajectory is firmly in the web and TypeScript/Node.js space. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT), IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle, or IoT/industrial domain knowledge.
strengths: Strong event-driven and distributed-systems background at scale (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/sec; Tencent: high-volume mail platform)
React and Node.js experience aligns with the role's advantageous skills
Broad exposure to architecture, open-source platform development, and agile practices across many technology contexts
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~7 months professionally at NI in 2014-2015 and one student thesis project) — far short of the 8-12 years of senior C++ development required
No demonstrated knowledge of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, DNP3, IEC 61850)
No industrial automation, embedded systems, or IoT domain experience; entire career is web/cloud/mobile-focused
